concepts.title: Intersection multiplicity
concepts.blurb: Counting intersections correctly requires multiplicity at each point.

concepts.title: Resultants and elimination
concepts.blurb: Resultants detect common roots and compute projected elimination conditions.

concepts.title: Bézout theorem
concepts.blurb: Plane curves of degrees $m,n$ meet in $mn$ points counting multiplicity over algebraic closure.

concepts.title: Cayley–Bacharach and associativity
concepts.blurb: Nine points of $C_1\cap C_2$ are so rigid that any cubic through eight passes through the ninth — the combinatorial identity that makes the chord–tangent group law associative.

concepts.title: Bézout in higher dimensions
concepts.blurb: For hypersurfaces of degrees $d_i$ meeting properly in $\mathbb{P}^n$, the total intersection length is $d_1\cdots d_n$, formalized as the Chow-ring product $[H_1]\cdots[H_n]=d_1\cdots d_n\cdot[\mathrm{pt}]$.
